_meta.purpose: Structured DBT skills for clinical cartridge design and NPC dialogue
_meta.note: Skill structure is common knowledge; specific worksheets are copyrighted. Game integration mappings are original.

modules.name: Core Mindfulness
modules.goal: Observe, describe, and participate in the present moment without judgment

what_skills.name: Observe
what_skills.instruction: Notice without reacting. Just watch thoughts and feelings come and go.
what_skills.game_mapping: A quest where the character must sit still and describe what they see/hear/feel before acting.
what_skills.npc_dialogue: What do you notice right now? Don't change it — just notice.

what_skills.name: Describe
what_skills.instruction: Put words on your experience. Label thoughts as thoughts, feelings as feelings.
what_skills.game_mapping: NPC asks the character to describe what they're experiencing in the game world.
what_skills.npc_dialogue: Can you put words to what your character is feeling right now?

what_skills.name: Participate
what_skills.instruction: Throw yourself completely into the activity. Become one with the experience.
what_skills.game_mapping: A quest that rewards full engagement — no half-measures, the character must commit.
what_skills.npc_dialogue: Your character is right here, right now. What do they want to do with this moment?

how_skills.name: Non-Judgmentally
how_skills.instruction: See but don't evaluate. Take each moment as it is, not as you wish it were.
how_skills.game_mapping: NPC companion who models accepting the world as it is without labeling good/bad.

how_skills.name: One-Mindfully
how_skills.instruction: Do one thing at a time. Focus on the single task at hand.
how_skills.game_mapping: Quests that require focused attention — rushing or multitasking leads to failure.

how_skills.name: Effectively
how_skills.instruction: Do what works. Focus on what's effective, not what's 'right' or 'fair'.
how_skills.game_mapping: A quest where the 'right' choice isn't the effective one — the character must be pragmatic.

wise_mind.concept: The synthesis of emotional mind and reasonable mind
wise_mind.instruction: Access your inner wisdom — the place where knowing and feeling meet.
wise_mind.game_mapping: A mentor NPC who asks 'What does the wise part of your character think?'

modules.name: Distress Tolerance
modules.goal: Survive crisis moments without making things worse

crisis_survival.name: TIPP

components.instruction: Hold ice, splash cold water on face — activates dive reflex, calms nervous system
Run, jump, do push-ups — burns adrenaline
Breathe out longer than in — activates parasympathetic
Tense and release each muscle group

crisis_survival.game_mapping: Rest point mechanic — character does a TIPP activity to restore calm before continuing.

crisis_survival.name: STOP

components.instruction: Freeze. Don't react.
Literally or figuratively step away.
What's happening inside and outside?
Act with awareness, not on impulse.

crisis_survival.game_mapping: When a game event triggers emotional reaction, NPC suggests STOP before the character acts.

crisis_survival.name: Pros and Cons
crisis_survival.instruction: List pros and cons of both tolerating the distress AND acting on the urge.
crisis_survival.game_mapping: Quest choice where the character weighs options — a journal entry comparing paths.

crisis_survival.name: Distract with WISE MIND ACCEPTS
crisis_survival.game_mapping: When emotional intensity peaks, an NPC offers an interesting side activity (distraction with purpose).

reality_acceptance.name: Radical Acceptance
reality_acceptance.instruction: Accept reality completely — not approving, just acknowledging what is.
reality_acceptance.game_mapping: A quest where the character cannot change the outcome — the lesson is accepting it.
reality_acceptance.npc_dialogue: Some things in this world can't be changed. What can your character do with what is?

reality_acceptance.name: Turning the Mind
reality_acceptance.instruction: Choose over and over to accept. Each time rejection comes back, turn toward acceptance again.
reality_acceptance.game_mapping: A recurring game mechanic where the character faces the same acceptance choice repeatedly.

reality_acceptance.name: Willingness vs. Willfulness
reality_acceptance.instruction: Willingness = doing what works, participating in life. Willfulness = refusing to tolerate, giving up.
reality_acceptance.game_mapping: NPC who models willingness — keeps going despite difficulty, versus an NPC who is willful and stuck.

modules.name: Emotion Regulation
modules.goal: Understand, reduce vulnerability to, and change unwanted emotions

skills.name: ABC PLEASE

components.instruction: Do pleasant activities — short-term and long-term
Do one thing each day that builds competence
Plan for difficult situations before they happen
Take care of health
Eat regularly, not too much or too little
Stay away from drugs and alcohol
Get enough sleep
Move your body regularly

skills.game_mapping: Game rewards routine actions (sleep, eat, practice) as part of the daily narrative cycle.

skills.name: Opposite Action
skills.instruction: When the emotion doesn't fit the facts, act OPPOSITE to the emotional urge.
skills.game_mapping: A quest where the obvious choice matches the emotion but the better choice is the opposite.

skills.name: Check the Facts
skills.instruction: Ask: What event triggered the emotion? What are my interpretations? Are they facts or assumptions?
skills.game_mapping: NPC who asks clarifying questions before the character reacts to a game event.

modules.name: Interpersonal Effectiveness
modules.goal: Get what you need from relationships while maintaining self-respect and the relationship

skills.name: DEAR MAN

components.instruction: Describe the situation factually
Express feelings and opinions about the situation
Ask for what you want or say no clearly
Explain why meeting your request is positive
Stay focused on your objective, don't get distracted
Use confident tone and posture
Be willing to give to get, offer alternatives

skills.game_mapping: NPC dialogue choices structured as DEAR MAN steps — the character practices assertive communication.

skills.name: GIVE

components.instruction: Be gentle — no attacks, threats, or judging
Act interested — listen, don't interrupt
Validate the other person's feelings and thoughts
Use a little humor, be light

skills.game_mapping: Companion NPC relationship mechanic — using GIVE builds bond level, violating it decreases trust.

skills.name: FAST

components.instruction: Be fair to yourself AND the other person
Don't over-apologize or apologize for existing
Don't sell out your values to get what you want
Don't lie, don't act helpless, don't exaggerate

skills.game_mapping: Quest choices where the character must balance getting what they want with maintaining integrity.
